title: Lipoprotein Cholesterol Distribution Assay
author: Sangderk Lee
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.izhcf36

steps.title: Blood is collected from mice in...
steps.instruction: Blood is collected from mice in EDTA coated tubes by cardiac puncture and plasma was isolated by centrifugation.

steps.title: An aliquot of plasma is diluted to 0
steps.instruction: An aliquot of plasma is diluted to 0.5 µg total cholesterol/µL in 0.9% NaCl, 0.05% EDTA/NaN 3 and centrifuged at 2000xg for 10 minutes to remove any particulate debris.

steps.title: The supernatant is transferred to a...
steps.instruction: The supernatant is transferred to a glass insert contained in a GC vial.

steps.title: After loading the vial into an...
steps.instruction: After loading the vial into an autosampler set at 4°C (Agilent Technologies, G1329A), 40 µL of sample is injected onto a Superose 6 10/300 (GE Healthcare Life Sciences) chromatography column.

steps.title: Under control of an isocratic pump...
steps.instruction: Under control of an isocratic pump (Agilent Technologies, G1310A/B), the sample is separated at a flow rate of 0.4 ml/min with eluent containing 0.9% NaCl, 0.05% EDTA/NaN 3 .

steps.title: Column effluent is mixed with total...
steps.instruction: Column effluent is mixed with total cholesterol enzymatic reagent (Pointe Scientific) running at a flow rate of 0.125 mL/min and the mixture is passed through a knitted reaction coil (Aura Industries Inc., EPOCOD) in a 37°C H 2 O jacket.

steps.title: The absorbance of the reaction mixture...
steps.instruction: The absorbance of the reaction mixture is read at 500 nm using a variable wavelength detector (Agilent Technologies, G1314F).

steps.title: The signal is subsequently integrated using...
steps.instruction: The signal is subsequently integrated using Agilent Open LAB Software Suite (Agilent Technologies).

steps.title: VLDL, LDL, and HDL cholesterol concentrations...
steps.instruction: VLDL, LDL, and HDL cholesterol concentrations are calculated by multiplying the total plasma cholesterol concentration by the cholesterol percentage within the elution region for each lipoprotein class.
